summary: The team is sprinting on the China Going-Global report and Intelligent China program launch, with Asia Start campaigns running dual-track and SEO prioritization shifting toward these flagship initiatives over US partnerships.
summaryDetail: The top priority is the 'New Chinese Playbook for Going Global' report with IE Business School, targeting early September completion for Singapore events; the team is translating the Chinese report, identifying op-ed contributors, and finalizing the one-pager for the president this week. Concurrently, the Intelligent China program webpage must go live this week, with immediate SEO and SCM campaigns starting, while Asia Start is running two parallel routes (AI and traditional) for the first month. Urgent this week: Joseph needs to provide the conversion tracking brief and draft SEO target areas, Julie must send the Asia Start spotlight article, and Xuan must follow up with social media agencies—Media Minds is the likely pick. Upcoming: menu categorization feedback is due, the Shun Tian Shu press release needs adjustment, and the team should prepare for the Tech in Asia meeting on the report launch.

meetings.title: SEO & Program Prioritization Strategy
meetings.summary: Team discusses SEO strategy to improve CKGSB's search ranking for programs in AI, EVs, and smart manufacturing in China, prioritizing Asia Star and Intelligent China programs over US partnerships.

meetings.title: Marketing Team Meeting - June 29, 2026
meetings.summary: Team discussed feedback on menu categorization, social media agency search, IE report translation, and various ongoing projects including brochures and press releases.

meetings.title: New Chinese Playbook Report Planning
meetings.summary: Team discusses joint report with CKGSB on Chinese companies globalizing to Southeast Asia, including op-eds, case studies, and launch at Tech in Asia or Tyson events in Singapore.

meetings.title: Asia Start & New Program Planning
meetings.summary: Discussed Asia Start campaign with two routes (AI vs traditional) and introduced a new in-depth program 'Intelligent China' targeting foreigners, with module one in Beijing in December.

meetings.title: EU-China Cooperation Discussion
meetings.summary: Discussion on EU-China relations, focusing on geopolitical tensions, de-risking, and the role of companies versus governments in shaping market cooperation.

actionItems.detail: Xuan to get back to Carnegie account manager today and has a call with Impactable tomorrow night.

actionItems.detail: Confirm if report can be launched at their event.

decisions.text: Prioritize Asia Star and Intelligent China programs over US partnerships for initial SEO focus.
Intelligent China webpage to be launched this week; SEO efforts to start immediately.
Menu should be categorized by topics, highlighting professors, publications, and research.
Only one press release for Shun Tian Shu is needed; either incorporate the 10 points or turn it into a 10-point article.
Media Minds is likely the best social media agency given budget constraints and time.
IE report translation should not exceed 50% of the original Chinese report.
Report will be jointly published by CKGSB and IE Business School.
Working title: 'The New Chinese Playbook for Going Global'.
Target completion: early September for Singapore events.
Asia Start will run two parallel routes for the first month: AI (Joseph) and traditional (Daisy).
Intelligent China program will focus on module one (Beijing) for initial marketing.
Use Temu as the second case study for the IE report instead of halocoko
Place key findings at the beginning of the IE report
Professor Li Wei confirmed support for the 'China's New Playbook for Going Global' report plan
decisions.meetingTitle: Re: 2026年英文报告: China’s New Playbook for Going Global
